title: evansia lacustris
side_effects: Evansia lacustris can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extract can lead to dizziness, nausea, and even kidney damage. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin lesions, blisters, and permanent scarring. If ingested in large quantities, evansia lacustris can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain.
